topic.what: Hardware-assisted phase-decoupled framework for secure federated learning in IoT using ARM TrustZone
topic.how: Decouples cryptographic setup (DH key exchange, Shamir secret sharing) from active training phase by executing setup during device idle windows, enabling single-round gradient transmission with TEE-isolated key storage
topic.why_matters: Existing SMPC-based secure aggregation imposes prohibitive O(N^2) synchronous communication overhead during latency-critical IoT training rounds, blocking PPML adoption in resource-constrained edge environments

characteristics.what_it_measures: Gradient inversion attack fidelity (PSNR) on masked vs plaintext gradients
characteristics.evidence: Geiping et al. gradient inversion attack on small CNN, CIFAR-10
characteristics.context: Reconstruction collapses to random noise (PSNR≈8 dB) matching independent uniform image baseline

characteristics.what_it_measures: Active-phase aggregation latency improvement vs synchronous SecAgg
characteristics.evidence: Rock Pi 4 devices, 20 clients, small CNN on CIFAR-10
characteristics.context: Active-phase latency reduction compared to O(N^2) synchronous key-agreement baseline
Includes fixed ~39 ms RPMB flush for rollback protection; scales with model dimension

characteristics.what_it_measures: End-to-end active-phase latency under k mid-round dropouts
characteristics.evidence: Rock Pi 4, N=20, small CNN, t=13 threshold
characteristics.context: O(1) scaling with k due to ~100 ms fixed timeout; 32-byte Shamir shares negligible vs gradient transmission

characteristics.vs_baseline: Beaver triple precomputation (~20 MB for 50 rounds)
characteristics.context: Independent of model dimension D and training horizon R; includes (N-1)×32 byte DH secrets, 16-byte AES-GCM tag, 4-byte counter

characteristics.evidence: Geiping et al. gradient inversion attack, CIFAR-10
characteristics.context: SSIM matches random noise baseline; angular decorrelation of gradient direction defeats iterative optimization

novelty: Phase-decoupled DH key establishment exploiting IoT idle/active duty cycles
TEE-minimalism: 632-byte Secure World footprint independent of model dimension
Hardware-isolated OS-compromise resistance for pairwise PRG seeds
Hardware-backed monotonic counter preventing mask reuse/rollback attacks
open_problems: Malicious server robustness via commit-and-prove techniques
Hardware-backed certificate attestation for peer authentication
Cross-device selection bias under systematic non-IID dropout
